description: Easy - Traditional Army, Marius's Background & Numidia

questions.question: What was required for Roman citizens to serve in the traditional Roman army before Marius's reforms?
options: Completion of a formal military academy
Only patricians could serve in the legions
Ownership of property that met a minimum value threshold
All citizens were required to serve regardless of their economic status

questions.question: What does the term 'novus homo' (new man) mean in Roman politics?
options: A young senator under the age of thirty
A politician with no senatorial ancestors
A foreigner who gained Roman citizenship
A military commander without political experience

questions.question: Where was Gaius Marius born?
options: Rome
Carthage
Numidia
Arpinum

questions.question: Who was Jugurtha?
options: A Roman general who fought against the Germanic tribes
A Carthaginian commander during the Third Punic War
A tribune who proposed land reform in Rome
A Numidian prince who seized the kingdom by eliminating his rivals

questions.question: Where was the kingdom of Numidia located?
options: On the Iberian Peninsula
In what is now Algeria and western Tunisia
In southern Gaul
Along the eastern Mediterranean coast

questions.question: Why did the Jugurthine War become a scandal in Rome?
options: Marius refused to fight against Jugurtha
The Senate voted to ally with Jugurtha against Carthage
Roman commanders repeatedly accepted bribes from Jugurtha
Roman soldiers mutinied and joined Jugurtha's forces

questions.question: What was the aquila?
options: A type of heavy javelin used by Roman soldiers
The military oath sworn by soldiers
A Roman warship design
The silver eagle standard that Marius gave to each legion

questions.question: Who were the Cimbri and Teutones?
options: Italian allies who revolted against Rome
Germanic tribes migrating from near the Baltic Sea
Carthaginian mercenaries
Greek kingdoms in the eastern Mediterranean

questions.question: What happened at the Battle of Arausio in 105 BCE?
options: Marius defeated Jugurtha and ended the Numidian War
The Teutones were destroyed by Roman forces
Two Roman armies were annihilated by the Cimbri
Sulla captured Jugurtha through negotiation

questions.question: What title was given to Marius after defeating the Germanic tribes?
options: Dictator perpetuo
Princeps senatus
Third founder of Rome
Pater patriae
